title: The Squid Game

content.problemStatement:
"Let the games begin."

Squid Game has become a blockbuster hit and the frontman is now finding it difficult to accommodate all the participants in Squid Game 2.0. So, he decided that he will allow only those participants who could solve the following problem.

There are a total of NNN players who are competing in the Squid Game, numbered from 111 to NNN. When the ithi^{\text{th}}ith player gets eliminated from the game, AiA_iAi​ amount of money is added to the prize pool. The game is played until N−1N-1N−1 players get eliminated, and the only player left is declared as the winner. The winner gets all the money present in the prize pool.

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN elements, where AiA_iAi​ denotes the prize money added to the prize pool when the ithi^{\text{th}}ith player gets eliminated from the game. Find the maximum prize that the winner can get, given that you can choose any player to be the winner.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the number of players.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the amount of money added to the prize pool when the ithi^{\text{th}}ith (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N) player dies.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the maximum prize that the winner can get, given that you can choose any player to be the winner.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of players. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, where Ai is the amount added to the prize pool when the i‑th player is eliminated.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible prize the winner can obtain.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total sum = 6, minimum = 1, answer = 6‑1 = 5.\nTest case 2: total sum = 5, minimum = 1, answer = 5‑1 = 4.\nTest case 3: total sum = 21, minimum = 1, answer = 21‑1 = 20.

solution.approach: The total money added to the prize pool is the sum of all Ai. If we decide that player i will be the winner, his own Ai will never be added because he never gets eliminated. Hence the prize for that choice equals totalSum - Ai. To maximise the prize we simply subtract the smallest Ai from the total sum. The algorithm therefore scans the array once, keeping track of the sum and the minimum element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
